cpuOverhead.description: vSphere hypervisor CPU overhead per host
cpuOverhead.notes: ESXi kernel and vCenter agent consume ~5% of host CPU

memoryOverhead.description: Memory reserved for hypervisor and per-VM overhead
memoryOverhead.notes: ESXi reserves ~2GB + ~100MB per VM for VM overhead

thin.name: Thin Provisioned
thin.description: Space allocated on demand, plan for 50% growth

snapshotOverhead.description: Snapshots consume ~15% additional space on average

replicationOverhead.description: vSphere Replication stores offsite, no local overhead

cpuOverhead.description: Hyper-V hypervisor CPU overhead per host
cpuOverhead.notes: Hyper-V has lower CPU overhead than VMware (~3%)

memoryOverhead.description: Memory reserved for parent partition and per-VM overhead

dynamic.name: Dynamic VHDX
dynamic.description: Space allocated on demand, plan for 50% growth

snapshotOverhead.description: Checkpoints consume ~15% additional space on average

replicationOverhead.description: Hyper-V Replica stores full copy locally (2× space)

datacenter.description: Windows Server Datacenter: 16-core minimum per server, unlimited VMs

standard.description: Windows Server Standard: 16-core minimum per server, 2 VMs per license

memoryOverhead.description: Memory reserved for host OS and per-VM overhead

qcow2.name: QCOW2
qcow2.description: Space allocated on demand, plan for 50% growth

snapshotOverhead.description: QCOW2 snapshots consume ~15% additional space on average

replicationOverhead.description: Proxmox replication stores offsite, no local overhead

memoryOverhead.description: Memory reserved for dom0 and per-VM overhead

thin.name: Thin Provisioned VDI
thin.description: Space allocated on demand, plan for 50% growth

snapshotOverhead.description: Snapshots consume ~15% additional space on average

replicationOverhead.description: XCP-ng replication stores offsite, no local overhead
